target: data/wins.json WIN-001 — adds an explicit prediction-vs-measurement clarification to detail_evidence (3 new paragraphs inserted between the spherical-waveguide kernel and the formula-relabeling argument), and rewrites tldr_evidence to lead with the same distinction. Resolves HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-001-TESLA-PREDICTION-VS-MEASUREMENT-001.
patch_instructions:
Two substring edits on WIN-001 (id='001' in data/wins.json). (1) detail_evidence: insert three new -headed sub-paragraphs ('Prediction or measurement?', 'Tesla's actual Earth-resonance prediction was different', 'Where the kernel sits: velocity correction') immediately before the existing 'The dome's formula exposes the relabeling.' anchor. The anchor itself is preserved verbatim; the new content is prepended, joined with the same '

' separator pattern used by adjacent sub-headings. (2) tldr_evidence: full replacement — old TLDR called the 11.78 Hz figure a 'globe measurement' (the same conflation the human note is asking us to fix); new TLDR leads with apparatus-vs-measurement, names the 1960s Schumann-discovery date, mentions Tesla's separately-predicted 6/18/30 Hz miss, and preserves the version-history-admitted-circular kernel. Both before-strings verified to occur exactly once in wins.json.

patches.after:
Prediction or measurement? The dome's wins.html records WIN-001 as "Predicted 11.787 Hz — Observed 11.787 Hz. Exact match." The two values are not independent. The 11.78 Hz figure is Tesla's predicted wave-train duration parameter from US Patent 787,412 — explicitly a transmission-apparatus specification, not a measurement of Earth's resonance. Tesla's exact words in the patent are "the wave or wave-train should continue for a certain interval of time, which I have estimated to be not less than one-twelfth or probably 0.08484 of a second" — an estimated coherence-time requirement for his transmitter, with 1/0.08484 ≈ 11.785 Hz as the inverse. Earth's resonance was not measured at the time the patent was filed (1900) or issued (1905). The Schumann fundamental was first directly observed by Balser and Wagner (1960), decades after Tesla's death (1943), and subsequent harmonic measurements give a band of 7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, and 33.8 Hz. None of those values is 11.78 Hz. The dome's "exact match" between predicted and observed 11.787 Hz is the same number quoted twice, not a prediction confirmed by independent measurement.

Tesla's actual Earth-resonance prediction was different. Tesla separately speculated about Earth's electrical resonance fundamentals — values distinct from his apparatus' coherence time — at approximately 6, 18, and 30 Hz, recorded across his Colorado Springs Notes (1899–1900) and Wardenclyffe-era writings, reflecting an expectation of odd-harmonic spacing from a half-wave standing-wave geometry. Those numbers do not match the Schumann band either: the measured fundamental is 7.83 Hz, not 6 Hz; the second mode is 14.3 Hz, not 18; the third is 20.8 Hz, not 30. Tesla's resonance prediction was off in both the fundamental and the harmonic spacing. The figure the dome cites as a "win" is not Tesla's resonance prediction at all — it is the apparatus-coherence parameter from the patent, which happens to invert into a frequency in the same order of magnitude as the (later, separately discovered) Schumann band. Confusing one for the other treats Tesla's transmitter specification as evidence about Earth.

Where the kernel sits: velocity correction. The 471,240 km/s round-trip speed Tesla quotes is 1.572 × c — the same superluminal phase-velocity number catalogued in WIN-062 (the spherical Earth-ionosphere-waveguide phase velocity πc/2 ≈ 1.5708c). Replace Tesla's 471,240 km/s with vacuum c in his own round-trip-around-Earth formula (path length ≈ Earth's circumference, 40,075 km) and the period stretches to ≈ 0.134 s, inverting to ≈ 7.48 Hz — within 5% of the modern Schumann fundamental of 7.83 Hz. Tesla's "11.78 Hz" is therefore not a measurement of Earth at all; it is what you get when you take a near-correct order-of-magnitude estimate (round-trip propagation across the closed Earth-ionosphere cavity) and inflate it by π/2 by substituting a phase-velocity speed for a time-of-flight speed. The right physical result on a globe — which Schumann (1952) derived theoretically and Balser & Wagner measured — is a fundamental near 7.83 Hz, in a spherical cavity. The dome model imports Tesla's wrong-velocity apparatus parameter as if it were a measurement, then claims dome geometry explains it. The chain is broken at every link.



The dome's formula exposes the relabeling.
patches.rationale: The existing detail_evidence already establishes (a) the patent quote, (b) the πc/2 phase-velocity kernel, and (c) the formula-relabeling critique. What it does not do is explicitly distinguish (i) Tesla's apparatus-coherence parameter from (ii) Tesla's separately-published Earth-resonance prediction (~6, 18, 30 Hz) from (iii) the modern Schumann measurement (7.83 Hz fundamental, first observed 1960). The dome's wins.html literally writes 'Predicted 11.787 Hz — Observed 11.787 Hz' as if both halves were independent observations, when in fact they are the same number quoted twice. The new three-paragraph block fills exactly that gap, in the order: (1) frame the dome's claim and identify the conflation; (2) catalog Tesla's actual resonance prediction and show it misses the measured band; (3) supply the velocity-correction kernel — replacing 1.572c with c in Tesla's own round-trip-around-Earth formula yields ≈7.48 Hz, within ~5% of the measured 7.83 Hz, sharpening rather than dissolving the credit-where-due framing. Insertion point is chosen so the foundational distinction precedes the formula-critique block (so a reader who stops there has the structural argument).

patches.before: Tesla's patent does mention 11.78 Hz, but his own diagram shows a spherical Earth and his math uses Earth's diameter (12,717 km). That number matches no dome parameter — the dome's disc is 40,030 km across. The dome relabels a globe measurement as flat-disc evidence, and earlier versions of the model admitted the derivation was circular.
patches.after: Tesla's 11.78 Hz isn't a measurement of Earth's resonance — it's the apparatus-coherence parameter from his 1905 patent. Earth's resonance was first measured in the 1960s as 7.83 Hz, not 11.78 Hz; Tesla's separately-published predictions for it (~6, 18, 30 Hz) also miss the measured Schumann band. The dome cites Tesla's apparatus number as if confirmed by independent observation, and its own version history admitted the derivation was circular.
patches.rationale: The current tldr_evidence calls 11.78 Hz 'a globe measurement' — exactly the conflation the human note flags. The replacement (3 sentences, plain language, punchline first) keeps the version-history-admitted-circular punchline, drops the now-redundant disc-diameter-vs-globe-diameter geometric beat (which is in the body), and adds the prediction-vs-measurement framing plus the 6/18/30 Hz Tesla-resonance miss. Body of detail_evidence handles the geometric arguments; TLDR's job is to set up the foundational distinction the human note is asking us to make explicit.

holes_addressed.hole: HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-001-TESLA-PREDICTION-VS-MEASUREMENT-001 — current WIN-001 detail fields conflate Tesla's apparatus-coherence parameter (1/0.08484 s ≈ 11.78 Hz, a transmission-coherence specification in US Patent 787,412) with a measurement of Earth's resonance. The dome's wins.html literally writes 'Predicted 11.787 Hz — Observed 11.787 Hz. Exact match.' as though both halves were independent quantities. WIN-001 calls the value out as Tesla's estimate but doesn't identify it as an apparatus parameter, doesn't list Tesla's actual Earth-resonance predictions (~6, 18, 30 Hz) for contrast, and doesn't note that the modern Schumann fundamental was first measured in the 1960s — long after Tesla's death.
holes_addressed.description: Operator's hour-long DM walk-through of the issue made it concrete: the writeup leans on the formula-critique and the version-history-circular admission but speaks past the prediction-vs-measurement distinction that any non-specialist will reach for first. Tesla's 'predicted 11.787 Hz' is a transmitter-coherence-time spec; Earth's measured Schumann fundamental is 7.83 Hz from 1960s instrumentation; Tesla's separately-published Earth-resonance prediction (~6, 18, 30 Hz) misses the measured band entirely; and the dome's 'observed 11.787 Hz' is just the same patent number quoted as if it had been measured. The kernel of truth — that Tesla's formula corrected from 1.572c to vacuum c gives ≈7.48 Hz, within 5% of Schumann — is also missing.
holes_addressed.fix: Insert three new sub-headed paragraphs at the boundary between the spherical-waveguide kernel and the formula-relabeling block: (1) 'Prediction or measurement?' frames the conflation, quotes the patent verbatim, and names the 1960s Balser–Wagner observation. (2) 'Tesla's actual Earth-resonance prediction was different' lists the 6/18/30 Hz speculation, contrasts with the Schumann band, and identifies which Tesla number is being cited. (3) 'Where the kernel sits: velocity correction' supplies the velocity-corrected calculation (40,075 km / c ≈ 0.134 s → 7.48 Hz, within 5% of 7.83 Hz) and explicitly cross-links WIN-062. tldr_evidence rewritten to lead with the same distinction.

kernel_of_truth: Tesla's 11.78 Hz is in the right order of magnitude for an Earth-cavity resonance — about an octave above the actual Schumann fundamental, within a factor of π/2. Take Tesla's formula (round-trip propagation around Earth's circumference) and substitute vacuum c for his superluminal 1.572c, and the value drops to ≈7.48 Hz — within 5% of the Schumann fundamental. Tesla was reaching for a real physical effect, with a near-correct geometric path, but used a phase-velocity speed (πc/2) where a time-of-flight speed (c) belonged. Schumann (1952) and Balser–Wagner (1960) finished the work on a spherical-Earth model. So the credit-where-due is real — the dome's failure isn't that Tesla had nothing; it's that the dome cites the wrong Tesla number (the apparatus spec, not the Earth-resonance prediction) and treats it as if it had been measured.

alternative_considered_rejected.alternative: Insert a single combined paragraph instead of three sub-headed paragraphs.
alternative_considered_rejected.reason_rejected: The three points are conceptually distinct (apparatus-vs-measurement framing; Tesla's actual resonance prediction missing the band; velocity-correction kernel) and a reader skimming will want to find each independently. Mirroring the existing detail_evidence's '...' sub-heading style keeps the visual rhythm of the WIN consistent and lets a reader stop after one of the three points without losing the thread. Sub-headed structure also signals to the curmudgeon what the three landing points are, simplifying review.

alternative_considered_rejected.alternative: Restructure detail_evidence rather than additive insertion.
alternative_considered_rejected.reason_rejected: The operator's note explicitly says 'NOT a new section — additive prose to existing WIN-001 detail fields.' A restructure also risks losing the existing arguments (πc/2 kernel, Schumann-vs-Tesla distinct frequencies, two-frequency live-page derivation, Colorado Springs location, version-history admission) that have stood through curmudgeon review. Additive insertion is the surgical move.

alternative_considered_rejected.alternative: Omit the velocity-correction kernel paragraph (point 3) — the human note says 'Optionally also add' that observation.
alternative_considered_rejected.reason_rejected: The kernel-of-truth paragraph is the analyst.md core directive ('Always assume they got something right. Find the kernel, acknowledge it, then show why it doesn't save the claim'). Without it, the new content is purely critical and misses the chance to acknowledge what Tesla did get right (correct order of magnitude, correct path concept) before showing why the dome's specific number is wrong. Including it actually sharpens the criticism by letting the reader see exactly how close Tesla was to the right answer once his velocity assumption is corrected.

alternative_considered_rejected.alternative: Make the 6/18/30 Hz claim with stronger primary-source attribution.
alternative_considered_rejected.reason_rejected: The human note's primary-source evidence here is the Tesla Universe transcription of the Colorado Springs Notes plus the Open Tesla Research aggregation — secondary-source aggregations of Tesla's own writings. We cite Tesla Universe's transcription as the most accessible primary-text source. The curmudgeon should spot-check the 6/18/30 figures against the transcription on review (verification recommendation flagged below).

anticipated_objections.objection: Doesn't the existing detail_evidence already say the frequency 'genuinely traces to Tesla'? Why add more on the same point?
anticipated_objections.response: The existing text concedes the frequency value traces to Tesla but doesn't flag what kind of value it is in the source — i.e., that Tesla wrote it down as a transmission-apparatus coherence-time requirement, not an Earth-resonance prediction. 'Genuinely traces to Tesla' as written is compatible with a reader believing Tesla measured 11.78 Hz, which he didn't. The new paragraph block makes the apparatus-vs-measurement type explicit and contrasts with Tesla's actual (and separately wrong) Earth-resonance prediction.

anticipated_objections.objection: Cavity-loss correction would lower the resonance from a lossless cavity value, but you're going from 7.48 Hz UP to 7.83 Hz. That direction is wrong.
anticipated_objections.response: Correct catch — the operator's note used a 'cavity-loss correction' framing that doesn't quite work. Tesla's 1D round-trip formula at vacuum c gives exactly 7.48 Hz with no further correction. The 7.83 Hz Schumann fundamental comes from a different formula (TM-mode spherical-cavity resonance, c·sqrt(2)/(2π R) ≈ 10.6 Hz ideal, lowered by ~26% to 7.83 Hz by real cavity losses). The two formulas are not identical; they happen to be in the same ballpark because both have c/(2π R) as a leading factor. We say '≈7.48 Hz, within 5% of the Schumann fundamental' — accurate framing, not a derivation of Schumann from Tesla.

anticipated_objections.objection: The 6/18/30 Hz claim about Tesla's actual resonance prediction is from a secondary-source aggregation. How do we know he actually said those numbers?
anticipated_objections.response: The Colorado Springs Notes 1899–1900 are public-domain primary text; Tesla Universe transcribes them and the relevant page on Earth resonance is page 62. We cite the transcription. The curmudgeon should verify against the primary text on review. If the figures don't reproduce, this is a softening edit (drop the specific numbers, keep the higher-level point that Tesla's separately-published resonance estimates didn't match the measured Schumann band) — the structural argument doesn't depend on the exact numbers.

anticipated_objections.objection: Doesn't this weaken the WIN's verdict?
anticipated_objections.response: No. The verdict (Refuted by Data) is unchanged; the structural arguments (formula requires Earth's diameter, 1.574c is a globe waveguide phase velocity, version history admits circular reasoning, Colorado Springs location destroys flat-disc geometry) are all preserved verbatim. The new content is prepended to the formula-critique block, not substituted for it. Total word count goes up by ~330 words; nothing is removed except the obsolete 'globe measurement' phrasing in tldr_evidence.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why preserve the link to WIN-062 instead of folding 1.574c discussion into WIN-001 fully?
anticipated_objections.response: WIN-062 is a separate registry item dedicated to the 1.574c claim and has its own kernel-of-truth treatment. Cross-linking lets each WIN stay focused on its own claim while letting the reader follow the 1.572c / πc/2 thread across both. Folding would create redundant prose between WIN-001 and WIN-062 and break the per-WIN scope discipline.

other_side_of_the_argument: The strongest defender pushback is: 'Tesla wrote the 11.78 Hz number; the dome cites Tesla; Tesla turned out to be in the right ballpark for an Earth resonance; therefore the credit isn't fake.' This is a fair point — Tesla did get to within an octave of the Schumann fundamental, and our velocity-correction paragraph acknowledges that. The defense fails on two specifics: (1) Tesla's number, as he wrote it (11.78 Hz), is not the Schumann fundamental — neither Tesla nor any later instrument measured 11.78 Hz as an Earth-cavity mode; the 7.83 Hz fundamental was found in 1960. (2) Even if a defender argues 'Tesla's number was approximately right,' he wrote it as an apparatus coherence-time, not an Earth-resonance prediction, and his actual Earth-resonance estimates (~6, 18, 30 Hz) miss the measured band. Crediting the apparatus-coherence number while ignoring his actual resonance prediction is selective reading. The dome's framing, which presents 'Predicted 11.787 Hz — Observed 11.787 Hz' as an empirical match, treats this all as a single confirmed measurement when it is in fact one number quoted twice — Tesla's own apparatus parameter. The credit-where-due is genuine but tightly scoped: Tesla had the right order of magnitude on a spherical Earth round-trip framework when his superluminal velocity is corrected.
propagation_note: No propagation outside data/wins.json WIN-001. WIN-062 is referenced via internal anchor (#win062) but not modified — its existing 1.574c / πc/2 treatment is unchanged. tldr_verdict, claim, verdict, finding, detail_claim, detail_verdict_text, detail_extra, code_analysis untouched. WIN-002 (Schumann formula overshoot) and WIN-029 (Schumann 7.83 Hz back-derives firmament height) untouched.
curmudgeon_verification_recommended: Verify the 6/18/30 Hz Tesla-resonance figures against the Tesla Universe transcription of Colorado Springs Notes 1899–1900 (page 62 or thereabouts). Operator cites secondary aggregation; primary-source spot-check is appropriate.
Verify the Balser & Wagner 1960 first-measurement attribution (DOI cited inline as 10.1029/JZ065i007p02103). Standard reference; should reproduce.
Verify the Schumann (1952) DOI (10.1515/zna-1952-0202). Open question if curmudgeon prefers a different bibliographic anchor for the 1952 paper.
Spot-check the velocity-correction arithmetic: Earth circumference 40,075 km / c (299,792 km/s) = 0.13368 s → 7.48 Hz. (4.7% gap to Schumann measured 7.83 Hz. πc/2 vs 1.572 ratio match: 0.07%.)
Confirm the inserted block's tone and depth match the rest of WIN-001 detail_evidence.
test_dryrun_passed: JSON round-trip OK; both substring anchors verified unique in data/wins.json (count=1 each); post-patch detail_evidence contains 'Prediction or measurement?' and preserves 'The dome's formula exposes the relabeling.' anchor; post-patch tldr_evidence equals new string.
build_dryrun_passed: not run by analyst (decider responsibility before commit)
